headline: New York reaches 8 gigawatts of solar power capacity
meta_description: New York generates enough solar power to serve 1.3 million homes and businesses after reaching 8 gigawatts statewide.
summary_short: New York's solar capacity now powers over 1.3 million homes and businesses, marking significant renewable energy progress.
body:
New York has reached a renewable energy milestone: 8 gigawatts of installed solar power capacity statewide. That's enough to generate electricity for more than 1.3 million homes and businesses across the state. The growth represents years of investment in rooftop panels, solar farms, and community projects that have turned sunlight into reliable power.

This achievement shows that large-scale solar adoption is possible even in states with winters and varied seasons. As the capacity continues to grow, it reduces dependence on fossil fuels and moves the state closer to its clean energy goals, while creating jobs and lowering energy costs for families and businesses.
